Name: Alice
Age: 20
GPA: 3.8
---

---
phase1.explanation:
Object-Oriented Programming (OOP) is about modeling real-world things:

Class: blueprint/template (e.g., Student)
• Defines what properties (fields) and behaviors (methods) objects will have

Object: instance of a class (e.g., alice, bob)
• Actual thing with specific values

Fields: variables that hold data (name, age, gpa)

Methods: functions that define behavior (displayInfo)

Constructor: special method that creates objects
• Called with 'new' keyword
• Same name as class
• No return type

this keyword: refers to the current object
• Used to distinguish between field and parameter with same name

Key OOP concept: Classes are reusable blueprints!

phase2.explanation:
void Student(...) is a METHOD, not a CONSTRUCTOR!

Constructors:
• Have NO return type (not even void)
• Must have the SAME name as the class

Correct: Student(String name, int age, double gpa)

Mistake: void Student(String name, int age, double gpa) ← this is a method

The compiler can't find a constructor that takes parameters!

phase3.prompt:
Create a class called Book with:
• Fields: title (String), author (String), pages (int)
• Constructor that initializes all fields
• Method: displayInfo() that prints all book info

In main, create 2 Book objects and call displayInfo() on both.
dialogueHints: Fields should be instance variables (not static)
Constructor takes parameters and uses this.field = parameter
phase3.openingDialogue: Time to create your first class! The blueprint of the future~ 🏗️
